sources.title: Gold-Silver Ratio: The 80-50 Rule (gold-standard.org)
signal_ideas: Gold-Silver Ratio 80-50 Rule: Buy silver (or swap gold->silver) when GSR >= 80. Buy gold (or swap silver->gold) when GSR <= 50. Convert 10-20% of gold holdings when ratio exceeds 70. Monitor 50-60 range for shift back toward gold. Dollar-cost average rather than timing perfectly.
Computation: GSR = Price_of_Gold / Price_of_Silver. No technical indicator needed, just spot prices.

backtest_results.vs_buy_hold: 2.58x better than static gold hold (which returned 1438%)
backtest_results.notes: Only 5 trades in 25 years = very low frequency. Buy-and-hold gold had higher Sharpe (0.72) despite lower returns.

sources.actionable_for_system: Already have metals_cross_assets.py computing G/S ratio. Could add GSR-based signal with thresholds at 80/50. Very low frequency but strong mean-reversion thesis. Current GSR ~80 = silver accumulation zone.

sources.title: COT Commercial Index Signal (best-trading-platforms.com)
signal_ideas: Dual COT Commercial Index: Compute 6-month and 12-month Commercial Index (min-max normalization of commercial net positions). LONG when BOTH 6m and 12m index > 75. SHORT when BOTH 6m and 12m index < 25.
COT Index Formula: COT_Index = (currentNet - min(net, N)) / (max(net, N) - min(net, N)) * 100. N = 26 weeks (6m) and 52 weeks (12m).
Confirmation filters (need 2 of 3): (1) Weekly trend alignment, (2) Commercials buy + OI falls (long) or commercials sell + OI rises (short), (3) Sentiment < 25 (long) or > 75 (short).
Entry timing: daily chart RSI/momentum divergence + 12-day high/low breakout. Stop: below 3-day low. Signal validity: ~6 weeks.

backtest_results.validation: 3rd place 2017 World Championship of Futures Trading (real money)

sources.actionable_for_system: Already have COT signal module. Could enhance with dual-timeframe index (6m+12m) and the 75/25 threshold confirmation system. Would improve existing COT signal significantly.

sources.title: VIX Term Structure Regime Signal (volatilitybox.com)
signal_ideas: VIX/VIX3M Ratio regime detector: Below 0.95 = contango confirmed. 0.95-1.0 = transition zone (reduce positions 50%). Above 1.0 = backwardation confirmed.

VIX Futures Basis: Front-month - Spot VIX. >+2.0 = deep contango (risk-on). +0.5 to +2.0 = normal contango. 0 to +0.5 = flat/transition. <0 = backwardation (risk-off). Extremes >+4.0 or <-3.0 = mean-reversion opportunity.
Additional: VIX9D spike 20%+ above VIX = extreme near-term fear. M1-M2 spread compression toward zero precedes full inversion.
Re-entry rule: Wait 2-3 days after ratio drops below 1.0, enter at 50% size, scale to full at <0.90.

backtest_results.notes: No formal Sharpe/win rate. Contango occurs ~80% of the time.

data_requirements: VIX futures front-month and second-month prices
sources.actionable_for_system: Already have VIX Term Structure signal module. Could refine with the 0.95/1.0 ratio thresholds and the VIX9D spike confirmation. Useful as cross-asset risk regime overlay for all instruments.

sources.title: MVRV Z-Score Bitcoin Signal (multiple sources)
signal_ideas: MVRV Z-Score = (Market_Value - Realized_Value) / StdDev(Market_Value). Market Value = price * circulating supply. Realized Value = aggregate cost basis of all coins.
Thresholds: Z > 6.0 = extreme overvaluation (red zone, distribution risk). Z 3.5-6.0 = elevated risk (yellow zone). Z < 0.5 = accumulation opportunity (green zone). Z < 0 = deep value / capitulation.
TradingView strategy variant: Long entry at spread Z-Score crossing above 0.382, short entry below -0.382. 252-day lookback for Z-Score calculation.
BTC spends ~44% of time in green zone. Every major cycle top and bottom since 2013 marked by extreme Z-Score readings.
Confirm with: NUPL, STH SOPR < 1.0, Exchange Netflow (outflows = accumulation).

backtest_results.notes: No formal backtest metrics. Framework is zone-based rather than mechanical. Historical accuracy for identifying tops/bottoms described as very high but sample size is small (4-5 full cycles).

sources.actionable_for_system: Already have On-Chain BTC signal with MVRV Z-Score. Could refine thresholds based on these zone definitions (0.5/3.5/6.0). Current implementation may not use optimal thresholds.

sources.title: Hash Ribbons Bitcoin Buy Signal (Capriole Investments)
signal_ideas: Hash Ribbons: 1-month SMA of BTC Hash Rate vs 2-month SMA of BTC Hash Rate.
Miner capitulation: 1m SMA crosses below 2m SMA. Recovery: 1m SMA crosses back above 2m SMA.
Enhanced buy signal: After hash rate recovery (1m crosses above 2m), wait for 10-day price SMA to cross above 20-day price SMA. This momentum filter significantly reduces max drawdown.
Signal sequence: (1) Capitulation detected -> (2) Hash rate recovers -> (3) Price momentum confirms -> BUY.
SMA periods not critical -- the concept is robust across parameter choices.
data_requirements: Bitcoin hash rate (daily, from blockchain.com or Glassnode)
sources.actionable_for_system: Already have Hash Ribbons signal module (disabled). 89% win rate on 9 signals is compelling but very low frequency. Could activate as a rare high-conviction overlay. Data already available from on-chain sources.

sources.title: MSTR mNAV Premium/Discount Signal (bitcoinquant.co + bankless)
signal_ideas: mNAV = Market_Cap / BTC_NAV. Where BTC_NAV = BTC_Holdings * BTC_Price.
EV-based variant: Enterprise_Value / BTC_NAV (includes debt and preferred stock).

Signal zones: mNAV > 2.0 = extreme premium (historically preceded corrections). mNAV 1.0-2.0 = normal premium. mNAV < 1.0 = discount (rare, historically strong buy signal). Current: EV mNAV = 1.08, Market Cap mNAV = 0.83.
Davis Double Play: When mNAV < 1.0, potential for dual return from (1) BTC appreciation + (2) premium normalization.
mNAV peaked >3.0x in late 2024 during BTC bull run. Compressed to ~1.0 after BTC spot ETFs launched (structural premium evaporated).
data_requirements: MSTR market cap or stock price
sources.actionable_for_system: Not currently implemented. Simple to compute: just need MSTR price, shares outstanding, BTC holdings (updated quarterly from filings, ~528K BTC as of recent). Could add as MSTR-specific signal. mNAV < 1.0 is a clear buy signal.

sources.title: Precious Metals Intraday Bias Strategy (Benzinga/Unger Academy)
signal_ideas: Gold/Silver intraday bias: Night weakness (2:00 AM to late morning), afternoon rebound, then resumption of weakness next night.
Gold entry rules: Long at 10:00 AM NY time, short at 2:00 AM NY time. Positions alternate (closing prior position).
Pattern replicates across Gold, Silver, Platinum, and Copper.
15-year backtest (2010-2025) segmented into 3-year intervals shows pattern stability.
Limitation: Average trade size is modest due to short daily exposure.

backtest_results.notes: Positive equity curve shown but no specific Sharpe/win rate/drawdown numbers published. Pattern confirmed across all four metals.

sources.actionable_for_system: Already have Gold Overnight Bias and Intraday Seasonality signals (both disabled/pending). This validates the concept. Could activate overnight bias with these specific time windows (2AM/10AM NY). Metals loop runs 24/7 so timing is feasible.

sources.title: Copper-Gold Ratio Recession Indicator (gold-standard.org)
signal_ideas: Copper/Gold Ratio = Copper_Price_per_oz / Gold_Price_per_oz. Rising ratio = economic expansion. Falling ratio = recession risk.
Historical correlation with 10Y Treasury yields: ~0.85 (weakening in recent years).
Current ratio (2025): ~0.00077, lowest in 50 years. Extreme risk-off signal.
Key historical readings: 2008 crisis = 0.00110, 2020 COVID = 0.00084, 1980 stagflation = 0.00086.
QuantifiedStrategies: Buy signal when Cu/Au ratio < 0.19 for first time in 1 year. Positive results for 1-12 month holding periods.
Caveat: 2025 distorted by tariff-driven copper flows. False signals common around macro tremors.

backtest_results.notes: Positive forward returns after ratio drops below 0.19 threshold (1-12 month horizons). No formal Sharpe/win rate. Primarily a regime indicator, not a trade signal.

sources.actionable_for_system: Already have Copper/Gold Ratio in metals_cross_assets.py. Currently used as a component of Metals Cross-Asset signal. Could add standalone ratio threshold signal. Extreme current reading (50-year low) suggests structural gold demand.

sources.title: Gold vs Real Yields Inverse Correlation (longtermtrends.com)
signal_ideas: Gold-Real Yield inverse correlation: r = -0.82 (Erb & Harvey). Gold rises when real yields fall.
Real Yield = 10Y Treasury Nominal Rate - Expected Inflation (or TIPS yield directly).
Signal logic: When real yields turn negative, gold becomes attractive (opportunity cost of holding zero-yield gold drops). When real yields rise sharply, gold faces headwind.
Breakeven inflation momentum: If breakevens rise faster than nominal rates, real yields fall -> gold bullish.
Caveat: Post-March 2022, gold rose despite rising real yields (safe-haven demand override). Correlation is state-dependent.
sources.actionable_for_system: Already have Gold Real Yield Paradox signal (disabled). Already have Breakeven Inflation Momentum signal (disabled). This confirms both are valid concepts. The -0.82 correlation is strong. Could activate with FRED data (already used for Treasury Risk Rotation).

sources.title: Cross-Asset Time-Series Momentum I-XTSM (Wiley/academic)
signal_ideas: Improved Cross-Asset TSMOM (I-XTSM): Use GSCI industrial metal index as the cross-asset signal instead of bonds.
Signal: Past 1-month return of cross-asset predicts equity market direction (NOT the traditional 12-month lookback).
Regime identification: Use cross-asset return sign as regime indicator. When industrial metals had positive 1-month return, equity momentum strategies perform better.
Standard XTSM: Past bond returns predict equity, past equity returns predict bonds. Relationship exploits lead-lag across asset classes.
I-XTSM increases profitability substantially and avoids momentum crashes.

backtest_results.notes: I-XTSM increases profitability substantially vs standard TSMOM. Avoids momentum collapse. Full paper behind paywall - exact Sharpe/returns not accessible.

sources.actionable_for_system: Already have Cross-Asset TSMOM signal (disabled). The I-XTSM improvement (use 1-month industrial metals as cross-asset signal instead of 12-month bonds) is actionable. Could fetch GSCI metals index via yfinance and implement 1-month lookback variant.

sources.title: Central Bank Gold Buying as Leading Indicator (World Gold Council)
signal_ideas: Central bank net gold purchases: Monthly data from WGC. 2025 average: 26t/month. Feb 2026: 27t (in line with trend).
Key buyers with consecutive streaks: Czech Republic (36 consecutive months), China (16 months), Uzbekistan (5 months).
Signal logic: Sustained CB buying (>20t/month, multiple months) = structural floor under gold prices. Sharp drop in CB buying pace = price sensitivity, potential pullback.
YTD 2026 pace (31t through Feb) trails 2025 equivalent by 38%, suggesting price sensitivity at current elevated levels.
New entrants (Africa: Uganda, Kenya) starting domestic gold purchasing = broadening demand base.
data_requirements: WGC monthly central bank gold statistics (free from gold.org)
sources.actionable_for_system: Not currently implemented as a signal. Monthly frequency is very low. Could add as a slow-moving fundamental overlay: scrape WGC monthly data. When CB buying >30t/month for 3+ months, boost gold bullish bias. When CB buying drops below 15t/month, reduce bullish bias.

sources.title: Breakout Momentum with Volume Filter (luxalgo.com)
signal_ideas: Volume surge breakout: Volume must be 10%+ above 50-day MA to confirm breakout.
Gold ETF backtest: Average 0.86% gain over 20 days without filter, rising to 1.25% with volume filter.
Multi-timeframe RSI: 5-min RSI agreement with 1-hr RSI achieves 90%+ accuracy in backtests. Use adaptive thresholds (40/80 in strong uptrends instead of standard 30/70).
Bollinger Bands: Middle Band = 20-period SMA, Outer = +/- 2 sigma (adjustable 1.5-2.5 sigma). Position sizing scales to band width.

backtest_results.notes: Volume filter improves gold ETF breakout returns by 45%

sources.actionable_for_system: Already have Volume Confirmation signal (disabled) and Volume Flow signal (disabled). The volume surge threshold (10%+ above 50-day MA) is a specific, testable rule. Could use as confirmation filter for existing breakout signals.

sources.title: DXY-Gold Inverse Correlation Divergence Signal
signal_ideas: Gold-DXY correlation: typically -0.5 to -0.8. 60-day rolling correlation ~-0.45.
Divergence signal: When gold rises despite DXY strength (or vice versa), it signals strong underlying demand/supply beyond dollar dynamics.
2025 breakdown: Gold surged to $4,380 despite mixed DXY, driven by CB buying and geopolitical safe-haven.
Trading rule: If 30-day rolling Gold-DXY correlation turns positive (>0), it signals regime shift - gold is decoupled from dollar and driven by other factors (usually bullish for gold).
Cancel trade if gold doesn't react within 2-3 candles after dollar move, or if real yields move differently.
sources.actionable_for_system: Already have DXY Cross-Asset signal (disabled). The specific divergence idea (positive correlation = regime shift) is interesting. Could add as a binary overlay: when 30-day Gold-DXY correlation > 0 for 5+ days, flag as 'gold decoupled' regime and boost gold bullish signals.

sources.title: Silver Seasonality: December-February and July-August
signal_ideas: Silver seasonality best periods: December to February (industrial demand for new year), July to August.
Cause: Industrial silver users place orders at beginning of new year driving Dec-Feb demand.
Silver follows gold's seasonal patterns but with higher beta.
Calendar-based: Long silver Dec 1 - Feb 28, Long silver Jul 1 - Aug 31. Flat otherwise.
sources.actionable_for_system: Already have Intraday Seasonality signal (disabled) and Calendar signal (disabled). The Dec-Feb / Jul-Aug silver seasonal pattern is well-known. Could activate Calendar signal with these specific monthly windows for silver.

sources.title: VIX Backwardation Directional Signal Analysis (CBOE)
signal_ideas: VIX backwardation occurs less than 20% of the time since 2010.
Backwardation periods tend to be shorter than contango periods.
Major backwardation episodes correlated with: 2008 crisis, 2011 EU debt crisis, Q4 2018 selloff, 2020 pandemic.
CRITICAL CAVEAT (from CBOE themselves): 'Outside of some of the biggest and most memorable market selloffs the results don't pan out.'
Best stock performance often follows immediately after biggest selloffs. Using backwardation as a sell signal causes whipsaw.

backtest_results.notes: CBOE's own analysis suggests backwardation is NOT a reliable standalone sell signal. Works only for the largest crises. Many false signals.

sources.actionable_for_system: Validates caution in using VIX backwardation as a standalone signal. Better used as a regime overlay (risk-off context) rather than a trade signal. Current VIX Term Structure signal should weight this appropriately.

sources.title: Cross-Asset TSMOM Sharpe Enhancement (microalphas.com)
signal_ideas: Time-Series Momentum (TSMOM) across assets: Sharpe ratios 45% higher than single-asset momentum.
TSMOM performance is 70% above diversified buy-and-hold.
Lookback periods: 6-12 months for cross-asset momentum signals.
Machine learning models achieve 80.6% and 83.6% predictive accuracy on cross-asset signals.
Minimum data requirement: 3-5 years (750-1250 daily observations), ideally 5-8 years for multiple cycles.

backtest_results.notes: No specific absolute Sharpe ratio provided. Relative improvement metrics only.

data_requirements: Futures prices across equities, bonds, currencies, commodities
sources.actionable_for_system: Already have Cross-Asset TSMOM signal (disabled). The 45% Sharpe improvement over single-asset momentum is compelling. Could implement with existing multi-asset data. 6-12 month lookback aligns with existing infrastructure.

sources.title: COT Index Thresholds for Gold/Silver (market-bulls.com + barchart)
signal_ideas: COT Index Formula: COT_Index = (currentNet - min(net, N)) / (max(net, N) - min(net, N)) * 100.
Standard lookback periods: N = 26 weeks (6-month), N = 52 weeks (12-month), N = 156 weeks (36-month/3-year).
Commercial Index thresholds: Above 80 = commercials bullish (contrarian BUY). Below 20 = commercials bearish (contrarian SELL).
Large Speculator contrarian: Near 90+ = speculators very long (contrarian SELL). Near 10 or below = speculators very short (contrarian BUY).
MarketBulls provides both 6-month and 36-month COT Index for gold -- dual timeframe confirmation improves reliability.
data_requirements: CFTC weekly COT data (free from cftc.gov)
sources.actionable_for_system: Already have COT Positioning signal. Could enhance with the specific 80/20 threshold on the normalized index and add the dual-timeframe (6m + 36m) confirmation. The normalization formula is exactly what min-max scaling does. Easy to implement.

sources.title: Alpha Architect: Value, Momentum & Carry Across Asset Classes
signal_ideas: Commodity momentum: 12-month lookback, skip most recent month (12-1 momentum). Long top tercile, short bottom tercile.
Commodity carry: Based on futures roll yield (backwardation = positive carry = long signal).
Commodity value: Based on mean-reversion of real commodity prices over 5-year horizon.
Factor crowding in commodities can erode premiums. Monitor factor crowding metrics.
150+ years of momentum evidence across 40+ countries. Most robust factor across all asset classes.

backtest_results.notes: AlphaArchitect site blocked (403). Summary from search results: momentum is most robust factor across asset classes over 150+ years. Specific commodity Sharpe ratios not accessible.

data_requirements: 5-year real price history for value factor
Roll yield data for carry factor
sources.actionable_for_system: Trend Slope Momentum (disabled) could implement 12-1 momentum. Could add carry factor using futures roll yield data from Binance FAPI (already available). Value factor needs 5-year real price series -- feasible with existing data.
